## Answer: It is **NOT impossible** — a valid assignment exists.

### Proof by construction

Here's the available grid (11 positions, Room 2/Slot 3 blocked):

| | Slot 1 | Slot 2 | Slot 3 | Slot 4 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Room 1** | T6 | T5 | T3 | — |

### Constraint verification

| # | Constraint | Check |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Max 1 talk per room per slot | Each cell has at most 1 talk |
| 2 | T1, T2 not same slot | T1 → Slot 1, T2 → Slot 2 |
| 3 | T3, T4 same slot | Both in Slot 3 |
| 4 | T5 in Room 1 | T5 → Room 1, Slot 2 |

| 6 | T7 immediately before T8 | T7 → Slot 1, T8 → Slot 2 (consecutive) |
| 7 | Room 2 unavailable Slot 3 | Room 2/Slot 3 is empty |

All 8 talks are placed in only 3 of the 4 time slots, using 8 of the 11 available positions. Every constraint is satisfied simultaneously.
